     5→There are no instructions for setting up a development environment. The project has no package.json, build.gradle, or any dependency manifest file, and no documentation explaining what tools, SDKs, or runtimes are needed. - **Phase 1: Research** (3 subtasks)
  - Research Android SDK requirements and versions
  - Document IDE setup requirements
  - Identify common troubleshooting scenarios

- **Phase 2: Documentation** (6 subtasks)
  - Create SETUP.md with prerequisites
  - Document Android SDK installation
  - Document Android Studio IDE setup
  - Document project initialization
  - Add build and run instructions
  - Add troubleshooting section

- **Phase 3: Review** (2 subtasks)
  - Review documentation for completeness
  - Create README.md with reference to SETUP.md

### 🎯 Deliverables

- **SETUP.md** - Comprehensive Android development environment setup guide
- **README.md** - Project overview with link to setup guide
- **research_notes.md** - Supporting research documentation (in spec directory)

### ⚙️ Verification Strategy

- **Risk Level:** Trivial (documentation-only)
- **Validation:** Manual review of documentation quality
- **Tests Required:** None (not applicable for documentation)
- **Security Scanning:** Not required

1→# Android Development Environment Research Notes
     2→
     3→**Research Date:** March 24, 2026
     4→**Purpose:** Document current Android SDK requirements and recommended versions for development environment setup guide
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## 1. Android SDK API Levels
     9→
    10→### Current Requirements (2026)
    11→
    12→**Google Play Target API Requirements:**
    13→- **New apps and app updates:** Must target Android 15 (API level 35) or higher
    14→- **Special platforms:** Wear OS, Android Automotive OS, and Android TV apps must target Android 14 (API level 34) or higher
    15→- **Existing apps:** Must target Android 14 (API level 34) or higher to remain available to new users
    16→
    17→**Recommended API Levels for 2026:**
    18→- **Target SDK:** API level 35 (Android 15)
    19→- **Minimum SDK:** API level 24 (Android 7.0) for broad device compatibility
    20→- **Compile SDK:** API level 35 or latest available
    21→
    22→### Future SDK Release Plans
    23→- Google plans for one annual requirement each year tied to the major API level only
    24→- More frequent SDK releases with two releases planned in 2025
    25→- Q2: Major release with new developer APIs
    26→- Q4: Minor release with new developer APIs
    27→
    28→### Key API Level Information
    29→- Android will continue quarterly SDK releases but target API requirements remain annual
    30→- API level 36.1 is already supported by Android Gradle Plugin 9.1 and 9.2
    31→- Developers should stay current with the latest stable API level for new projects
    32→
    33→**Sources:**
    34→- [SDK Platform release notes](https://developer.android.com/tools/releases/platforms)
    35→- [API Levels](https://apilevels.com/)
    36→- [Target API level requirements for Google Play apps](https://support.google.com/googleplay/android-developer/answer/11926878?hl=en)
    37→- [Meet Google Play's target API level requirement](https://developer.android.com/google/play/requirements/target-sdk)
    38→
    39→---
    40→
    41→## 2. Java and Kotlin Version Requirements
    42→
    43→### Java Versions
    44→
    45→**Current Recommendations (2026):**
    46→- **Source Compatibility:** Java 17
    47→- **Target Compatibility:** Java 17
    48→- **JVM Target:** '17'
    49→
    50→**Java Version Support:**
    51→- Android Studio 3.0+ supports Java 8 language features
    52→- Android Studio supports using Java 11+ APIs without requiring a minimum API level
    53→- Java 17 is the current standard for Android development in 2026
    54→- Gradle 9.4.1 now supports Java 26 (latest)
    55→
    56→**Configuration:**
    57→```groovy
    58→android {
    59→    compileOptions {
    60→        sourceCompatibility JavaVersion.VERSION_17
    61→        targetCompatibility JavaVersion.VERSION_17
    62→    }
    63→}
    64→```
    65→
    66→### Kotlin Versions
    67→
    68→**Current Recommendations (2026):**
    69→- **Kotlin Version:** 2.3.20 or higher (latest stable as of 2026)
    70→- **Kotlin Gradle Plugin (KGP):** 2.2.10 or higher
    71→- **Built-in Kotlin:** Enabled by default in Android Gradle Plugin 9.0+
    72→
    73→**Key Kotlin Features:**
    74→- Android Gradle Plugin 9.0 includes built-in Kotlin support
    75→- No longer need to apply `org.jetbrains.kotlin.android` plugin separately for Android apps
    76→- Kotlin 2.x includes multiple release types: language releases, tooling releases, and bug fix releases
    77→- Android Studio provides full code completion for Kotlin
    78→
    79→**JVM Target Configuration:**
    80→```kotlin
    81→kotlinOptions {
    82→    jvmTarget = '17'
    83→}
    84→```
    85→
    86→**Sources:**
    87→- [Java versions in Android builds](https://developer.android.com/build/jdks)
    88→- [Android Studio Panda 2](https://developer.android.com/studio/releases)
    89→- [AGP, D8, and R8 versions required for Kotlin versions](https://developer.android.com/build/kotlin-support)
    90→- [Kotlin release process](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/releases.html)
    91→- [Update your Kotlin projects for Android Gradle Plugin 9.0](https://blog.jetbrains.com/kotlin/2026/01/update-your-projects-for-agp9/)
    92→
    93→---
    94→
    95→## 3. Gradle Version Requirements
    96→
    97→### Android Gradle Plugin (AGP)
    98→
    99→**Latest Versions (2026):**
   100→- **AGP 9.1.0:** Released March 2026
   101→- **AGP 9.2.0:** Preview/Alpha available
   102→- **AGP 9.0.1:** Stable, released January 2026
   103→
   104→**Version Support:**
   105→- AGP 9.0: Supports maximum API level 36.1
   106→- AGP 9.1: Supports maximum API level 36.1
   107→- AGP 9.2: Supports maximum API level 36.1
   108→
   109→**Key Features in AGP 9.0:**
   110→- Built-in Kotlin support enabled by default
   111→- Requires Kotlin Gradle Plugin (KGP) 2.2.10 or higher
   112→- No longer requires separate application of `org.jetbrains.kotlin.android` plugin
   113→- Opt-out features will be removed in AGP 10.0 (mid-2026)
   114→
   115→### Gradle Versions
   116→
   117→**Latest Stable (2026):**
   118→- **Gradle 9.4.1:** Released March 19, 2026 (first patch release for 9.4.0)
   119→- **Gradle 9.x:** Tested with Android Gradle Plugin versions 8.13 through 9.1.0-alpha04
   120→
   121→**Compatibility:**
   122→- Gradle 9.4.1 supports Java 26
   123→- For best performance, use the latest possible version of both Gradle and AGP
   124→- Gradle is continuously tested with the latest AGP versions
   125→
   126→**Recommended Configuration:**
   127→```gradle
   128→// Project-level build.gradle
   129→plugins {
   130→    id 'com.android.application' version '9.1.0' apply false
   131→}
   132→
   133→// gradle-wrapper.properties
   134→distributionUrl=https\://services.gradle.org/distributions/gradle-9.4.1-all.zip
   135→```
   136→
   137→**Important Migration Notes:**
   138→- Migration to AGP 9.0 features is becoming increasingly important
   139→- AGP 10.0 (mid-2026) will remove opt-out capabilities for AGP 9.0 features
   140→- Always update to the latest stable versions for security and performance
   141→
   142→**Sources:**
   143→- [Compatibility Matrix](https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/compatibility.html)
   144→- [About Android Gradle plugin](https://developer.android.com/build/releases/about-agp)
   145→- [Android Gradle plugin 9.0.1 (January 2026)](https://developer.android.com/build/releases/agp-9-0-0-release-notes)
   146→- [Android Gradle plugin 9.1.0 (March 2026)](https://developer.android.com/build/releases/agp-9-1-0-release-notes)
   147→- [Gradle Releases](https://gradle.org/releases/)
   148→- [Gradle 9.4.1 Release Notes](https://docs.gradle.org/current/release-notes.html)
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## 4. Android Studio Requirements
   153→
   154→### Latest Version (2026)
   155→
   156→**Current Stable Release:**
   157→- **Android Studio Panda 2** (2025.3.2)
   158→- Released March 2026
   159→- Codename: Panda
   160→- Version: 2025.3.2
   161→
   162→### System Requirements
   163→
   164→#### Minimum Hardware Requirements
   165→- **RAM:** 8GB minimum (official requirement)
   166→- **Disk Space:** 8GB minimum for IDE + Android SDK + Android Emulator system image
   167→- **Screen Resolution:** 1280 x 800 minimum
   168→
   169→#### Recommended Hardware Requirements
   170→- **RAM:** 16GB minimum for practical development, 32GB recommended
   171→  - 8GB can cause performance slowdowns with emulators
   172→  - Android Emulator alone consumes ~4GB RAM
   173→  - With 8GB, only 4GB remains for IDE, OS, and browser
   174→- **Disk Space:** 20GB+ recommended
   175→  - IDE installation: ~3GB
   176→  - Android SDK: ~5GB
   177→  - Emulator system images: ~2GB each
   178→  - Gradle cache and build artifacts: 5-10GB+
   179→- **CPU:** Multi-core processor (4+ cores recommended)
   180→- **SSD:** Solid State Drive strongly recommended for build performance
   181→
   182→#### Operating System Requirements
   183→- **Windows:** Windows 10/11 (64-bit)
   184→- **macOS:** macOS 11.0 (Big Sur) or higher
   185→- **Linux:** GNOME or KDE desktop
   186→  - Tested on Ubuntu 20.04 LTS or higher
   187→  - 64-bit distribution capable of running 32-bit applications
   188→
   189→### 2026 AI Features Requirements
   190→- **Gemini Agent Mode:** Requires adequate RAM (16GB min, 32GB recommended)
   191→- **Journeys Testing:** Storage-efficient but benefits from higher RAM
   192→- AI features remain storage-efficient but require adequate memory for optimal performance
   193→
   194→### IDE Features
   195→- **Built-in Languages:** Kotlin, Java, C/C++ with full code completion
   196→- **Emulator:** Built-in Android Emulator for testing (hardware acceleration recommended)
   197→- **SDK Manager:** Integrated SDK management
   198→- **AVD Manager:** Integrated Android Virtual Device management
   199→- **Version Control:** Git, GitHub, SVN integration
   200→- **Build System:** Gradle with built-in Kotlin support (AGP 9.0+)
   201→
   202→### Download Size
   203→- **IDE Download:** ~1.5-2GB
   204→- **Full SDK Download:** ~5-8GB
   205→- **Total First-Time Setup:** ~10-15GB download
   206→
   207→**Sources:**
   208→- [How Much RAM Does Android Studio Really Need? [2026 Expert Guide]](https://techozea.com/minimum-ram-android-studio/)
   209→- [Android Studio: Definition, Features, Installation, System Requirements](https://www.intelivita.com/blog/android-studio/)
   210→- [Download Android Studio & App Tools](https://developer.android.com/studio)
   211→- [Android Studio Release Updates: Android Studio Panda 2](https://androidstudio.googleblog.com/2026/03/android-studio-panda-2-202532-now.html)
   212→- [What are the system requirements for Android Studio?](https://clouddevs.com/android/system-requirements/)
   213→
   214→---
   215→
   216→## Summary: Recommended Development Environment (2026)
   217→
   218→### Software Versions
   219→- **Android Studio:** Panda 2 (2025.3.2) or latest stable
   220→- **Android SDK:** API level 35 (Android 15) - compile and target
   221→- **Minimum SDK:** API level 24 (Android 7.0) or higher for compatibility
   222→- **Java:** JDK 17 (minimum, Java 26 supported by Gradle 9.4.1)

1→# Create a development environment setup guide
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→There are no instructions for setting up a development environment. The project has no package.json, build.gradle, or any dependency manifest file, and no documentation explaining what tools, SDKs, or runtimes are needed. A new developer cloning this repo would find an empty project with no guidance on how to bootstrap development — what IDE to use, what SDK versions are required, or how to create the initial project scaffold.

     9→Developer onboarding friction is the biggest early-project risk. Without setup documentation, each new contributor wastes time figuring out prerequisites independently. For an Android/APK project, setup is particularly important due to Android SDK versioning, emulator configuration, and build tool requirements. 1→# Android Development Environment Setup Guide
     2→
     3→Complete guide for setting up your Android development environment for this project.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Table of Contents
     8→
     9→1. [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
    10→   - [Operating System Requirements](#operating-system-requirements)
    11→   - [Hardware Requirements](#hardware-requirements)
    12→   - [Software Requirements](#software-requirements)
    13→2. [Android SDK Installation](#android-sdk-installation)
    14→3. [Android Studio Setup](#android-studio-setup)
    15→4. [Project Initialization](#project-initialization)
    16→5. [Building and Running](#building-and-running)
    17→6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
    18→7. [Additional Resources](#additional-resources)
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Prerequisites
    23→
    24→Before you begin setting up your Android development environment, ensure your system meets the following requirements.
    25→
    26→### Operating System Requirements
    27→
    28→This project supports development on the following operating systems:
    29→
    30→#### Windows
    31→- **Version:** Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows 11 (64-bit)
    32→- **Notes:**
    33→  - 32-bit Windows is not supported
    34→  - Windows 7 and 8 are not supported
    35→
    36→#### macOS
    37→- **Version:** macOS 11.0 (Big Sur) or higher
    38→- **Supported Architectures:**
    39→  - Intel-based Macs
    40→  - Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) Macs
    41→- **Notes:** Hardware acceleration is built-in via macOS Hypervisor Framework
    42→
    43→#### Linux
    44→- **Distributions:**
    45→  - Ubuntu 20.04 LTS or higher (recommended)
    46→  - Other distributions with GNOME or KDE desktop
    47→- **Architecture:** 64-bit distribution capable of running 32-bit applications
    48→- **Notes:**
    49→  - Debian-based distributions are best supported
    50→  - Requires additional 32-bit libraries for Android SDK compatibility
    51→
    52→### Hardware Requirements
    53→
    54→#### Minimum Requirements
    55→- **RAM:** 8 GB
    56→- **Disk Space:** 20 GB of available disk space
    57→  - ~3 GB for Android Studio
    58→  - ~5 GB for Android SDK
    59→  - ~2 GB per emulator system image
    60→  - ~5-10 GB for Gradle cache and build artifacts
    61→- **CPU:** x86_64 CPU architecture; 2nd generation Intel Core or newer, or AMD CPU with support for Windows Hypervisor
    62→- **Screen Resolution:** 1280 x 800 minimum
    63→- **Storage Type:** HDD (Hard Disk Drive)
    64→
    65→#### Recommended Requirements
    66→- **RAM:** 16 GB minimum, 32 GB recommended
    67→  - With 8GB RAM, Android Emulator alone consumes ~4GB
    68→  - Only 4GB remains for IDE, OS, and browser with 8GB total
    69→  - 16GB provides comfortable development experience
    70→- **Disk Space:** 50 GB of available SSD space
    71→- **CPU:** Multi-core processor (4+ cores recommended)
    72→- **Storage Type:** SSD (Solid State Drive) - **strongly recommended**
    73→  - Builds are 3-5x faster on SSD vs HDD
    74→  - Significantly improves IDE performance and responsiveness
    75→
    76→#### Optimal Requirements (for large projects)
    77→- **RAM:** 32 GB or more
    78→- **Disk Space:** 100 GB+ of SSD space
    79→- **CPU:** 6+ core processor
    80→- **Storage Type:** NVMe SSD
    81→
    82→> **Note on RAM:** The official minimum is 8GB, but practical Android development with emulators requires 16GB for smooth performance. With 8GB, you may experience slowdowns and need to close other applications.
    83→
    84→### Software Requirements
    85→
    86→The following software components are required for Android development on this project:
    87→
    88→#### Android Studio
    89→- **Version:** Android Studio Panda 2 (2025.3.2) or latest stable release
    90→- **Download:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
    91→- **Size:** ~1.5-2 GB download, ~3 GB installed
    92→- **Features:**
    93→  - Integrated Development Environment (IDE)
    94→  - Built-in Android SDK Manager
    95→  - Built-in Android Emulator
    96→  - Kotlin and Java language support
    97→  - Gradle build system integration
    98→
    99→#### Java Development Kit (JDK)
   100→- **Version:** JDK 17 (minimum required)
   101→- **Compatibility:** Java 26 is supported by Gradle 9.4.1
   102→- **Source:**
   103→  - Android Studio includes Embedded JDK (recommended for IDE use)
   104→  - Alternatively, download from [Oracle](https://www.oracle.com/java/technologies/downloads/) or [Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/)
   105→- **Configuration:**
   106→  - Source Compatibility: Java 17
   107→  - Target Compatibility: Java 17
   108→  - JVM Target: 17
   109→
   110→#### Android SDK
   111→- **Target SDK:** API Level 35 (Android 15)
   112→  - Required for Google Play Store compliance in 2026
   113→- **Minimum SDK:** API Level 24 (Android 7.0)
   114→  - Provides ~95% device compatibility
   115→- **Compile SDK:** API Level 35 or latest available
   116→- **Build Tools:** Version 35.0.0 or higher
   117→- **Platform Tools:** Latest version (includes ADB, fastboot)
   118→
   119→#### Kotlin
   120→- **Version:** Kotlin 2.3.20 or higher
   121→- **Kotlin Gradle Plugin:** 2.2.10 or higher
   122→- **Notes:**
   123→  - Android Gradle Plugin 9.0+ includes built-in Kotlin support
   124→  - No need to apply `org.jetbrains.kotlin.android` plugin separately
   125→
   126→#### Gradle
   127→- **Version:** Gradle 9.4.1 or latest stable
   128→- **Distribution:** Use 'all' distribution (includes sources and documentation)
   129→- **Android Gradle Plugin (AGP):** Version 9.1.0 or latest stable
   130→
   131→#### Additional Tools
   132→
   133→**Git** (Version Control)
   134→- **Version:** 2.x or higher
   135→- **Download:** [git-scm.com](https://git-scm.com/)
   136→- **Purpose:** Source code management and version control
   137→
   138→**Android Emulator System Images**
   139→- **Recommended:**
   140→  - API 35 (Android 15) - x86_64 or ARM64 (for Apple Silicon)
   141→  - Google APIs or Google Play variant
   142→- **Size:** ~2 GB per system image
   143→
   144→**Hardware Acceleration** (for Android Emulator)
   145→- **Windows (Intel):** Intel HAXM (Hardware Accelerated Execution Manager)
   146→- **Windows (AMD):** Windows Hypervisor Platform
   147→- **macOS:** Built-in Hypervisor Framework (no additional installation)
   148→- **Linux:** KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine)
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## Android SDK Installation
   153→
   154→*This section will guide you through installing the Android SDK and required components.*
   155→
   156→**Status:** To be completed in subtask-2-2
   157→
   158→---
   159→
   160→## Android Studio Setup
   161→
   162→*This section will guide you through setting up Android Studio IDE, SDK Manager, and creating Android Virtual Devices (AVDs).*
   163→
   164→**Status:** To be completed in subtask-2-3
   165→
   166→---
   167→

1→# Android Development Environment Setup Guide
     2→
     3→Complete guide for setting up your Android development environment for this project.
     4→
     5→---
     6→
     7→## Table of Contents
     8→
     9→1. [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
    10→   - [Operating System Requirements](#operating-system-requirements)
    11→   - [Hardware Requirements](#hardware-requirements)
    12→   - [Software Requirements](#software-requirements)
    13→2. [Android SDK Installation](#android-sdk-installation)
    14→3. [Android Studio Setup](#android-studio-setup)
    15→4. [Project Initialization](#project-initialization)
    16→5. [Building and Running](#building-and-running)
    17→6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
    18→7. [Additional Resources](#additional-resources)
    19→
    20→---
    21→
    22→## Prerequisites
    23→
    24→Before you begin setting up your Android development environment, ensure your system meets the following requirements.
    25→
    26→### Operating System Requirements
    27→
    28→This project supports development on the following operating systems:
    29→
    30→#### Windows
    31→- **Version:** Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows 11 (64-bit)
    32→- **Notes:**
    33→  - 32-bit Windows is not supported
    34→  - Windows 7 and 8 are not supported
    35→
    36→#### macOS
    37→- **Version:** macOS 11.0 (Big Sur) or higher
    38→- **Supported Architectures:**
    39→  - Intel-based Macs
    40→  - Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) Macs
    41→- **Notes:** Hardware acceleration is built-in via macOS Hypervisor Framework
    42→
    43→#### Linux
    44→- **Distributions:**
    45→  - Ubuntu 20.04 LTS or higher (recommended)
    46→  - Other distributions with GNOME or KDE desktop
    47→- **Architecture:** 64-bit distribution capable of running 32-bit applications
    48→- **Notes:**
    49→  - Debian-based distributions are best supported
    50→  - Requires additional 32-bit libraries for Android SDK compatibility
    51→
    52→### Hardware Requirements
    53→
    54→#### Minimum Requirements
    55→- **RAM:** 8 GB
    56→- **Disk Space:** 20 GB of available disk space
    57→  - ~3 GB for Android Studio
    58→  - ~5 GB for Android SDK
    59→  - ~2 GB per emulator system image
    60→  - ~5-10 GB for Gradle cache and build artifacts
    61→- **CPU:** x86_64 CPU architecture; 2nd generation Intel Core or newer, or AMD CPU with support for Windows Hypervisor
    62→- **Screen Resolution:** 1280 x 800 minimum
    63→- **Storage Type:** HDD (Hard Disk Drive)
    64→
    65→#### Recommended Requirements
    66→- **RAM:** 16 GB minimum, 32 GB recommended
    67→  - With 8GB RAM, Android Emulator alone consumes ~4GB
    68→  - Only 4GB remains for IDE, OS, and browser with 8GB total
    69→  - 16GB provides comfortable development experience
    70→- **Disk Space:** 50 GB of available SSD space
    71→- **CPU:** Multi-core processor (4+ cores recommended)
    72→- **Storage Type:** SSD (Solid State Drive) - **strongly recommended**
    73→  - Builds are 3-5x faster on SSD vs HDD
    74→  - Significantly improves IDE performance and responsiveness
    75→
    76→#### Optimal Requirements (for large projects)
    77→- **RAM:** 32 GB or more
    78→- **Disk Space:** 100 GB+ of SSD space
    79→- **CPU:** 6+ core processor
    80→- **Storage Type:** NVMe SSD
    81→
    82→> **Note on RAM:** The official minimum is 8GB, but practical Android development with emulators requires 16GB for smooth performance. With 8GB, you may experience slowdowns and need to close other applications.
    83→
    84→### Software Requirements
    85→
    86→The following software components are required for Android development on this project:
    87→
    88→#### Android Studio
    89→- **Version:** Android Studio Panda 2 (2025.3.2) or latest stable release
    90→- **Download:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
    91→- **Size:** ~1.5-2 GB download, ~3 GB installed
    92→- **Features:**
    93→  - Integrated Development Environment (IDE)
    94→  - Built-in Android SDK Manager
    95→  - Built-in Android Emulator
    96→  - Kotlin and Java language support
    97→  - Gradle build system integration
    98→
    99→#### Java Development Kit (JDK)
   100→- **Version:** JDK 17 (minimum required)
   101→- **Compatibility:** Java 26 is supported by Gradle 9.4.1
   102→- **Source:**
   103→  - Android Studio includes Embedded JDK (recommended for IDE use)
   104→  - Alternatively, download from [Oracle](https://www.oracle.com/java/technologies/downloads/) or [Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/)
   105→- **Configuration:**
   106→  - Source Compatibility: Java 17
   107→  - Target Compatibility: Java 17
   108→  - JVM Target: 17
   109→
   110→#### Android SDK
   111→- **Target SDK:** API Level 35 (Android 15)
   112→  - Required for Google Play Store compliance in 2026
   113→- **Minimum SDK:** API Level 24 (Android 7.0)
   114→  - Provides ~95% device compatibility
   115→- **Compile SDK:** API Level 35 or latest available
   116→- **Build Tools:** Version 35.0.0 or higher
   117→- **Platform Tools:** Latest version (includes ADB, fastboot)
   118→
   119→#### Kotlin
   120→- **Version:** Kotlin 2.3.20 or higher
   121→- **Kotlin Gradle Plugin:** 2.2.10 or higher
   122→- **Notes:**
   123→  - Android Gradle Plugin 9.0+ includes built-in Kotlin support
   124→  - No need to apply `org.jetbrains.kotlin.android` plugin separately
   125→
   126→#### Gradle
   127→- **Version:** Gradle 9.4.1 or latest stable
   128→- **Distribution:** Use 'all' distribution (includes sources and documentation)
   129→- **Android Gradle Plugin (AGP):** Version 9.1.0 or latest stable
   130→
   131→#### Additional Tools
   132→
   133→**Git** (Version Control)
   134→- **Version:** 2.x or higher
   135→- **Download:** [git-scm.com](https://git-scm.com/)
   136→- **Purpose:** Source code management and version control
   137→
   138→**Android Emulator System Images**
   139→- **Recommended:**
   140→  - API 35 (Android 15) - x86_64 or ARM64 (for Apple Silicon)
   141→  - Google APIs or Google Play variant
   142→- **Size:** ~2 GB per system image
   143→
   144→**Hardware Acceleration** (for Android Emulator)
   145→- **Windows (Intel):** Intel HAXM (Hardware Accelerated Execution Manager)
   146→- **Windows (AMD):** Windows Hypervisor Platform
   147→- **macOS:** Built-in Hypervisor Framework (no additional installation)
   148→- **Linux:** KVM (Kernel-based Virtual Machine)
   149→
   150→---
   151→
   152→## Android SDK Installation
   153→
   154→This section provides comprehensive instructions for installing the Android SDK and required tools on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
   155→
   156→### Installation Methods
   157→
   158→There are two primary methods for installing the Android SDK:
   159→
   160→1. **Via Android Studio (Recommended)** - Android Studio includes the SDK Manager with a graphical interface
   161→2. **Command-line Tools Only** - For CI/CD environments or minimal installations
   162→
   163→**For most developers, we recommend installing via Android Studio** as covered in this guide. Command-line-only installation is covered in the [Alternative: Command-line Tools Installation](#alternative-command-line-tools-installation) section.
   164→
   165→---
   166→
   167→### Method 1: Install Android SDK via Android Studio (Recommended)
   168→
   169→#### Step 1: Download Android Studio
   170→
   171→Visit the official Android Studio download page and download the installer for your operating system:
   172→
   173→**Download Link:** [https://developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
   174→
   175→**Expected File Sizes:**
   176→- Windows: ~1.5 GB (.exe installer)
   177→- macOS: ~1.3 GB (.dmg image)
   178→- Linux: ~1.2 GB (.tar.gz archive)
   179→
   180→#### Step 2: Install Android Studio
   181→
   182→##### Windows Installation
   183→
   184→1. **Run the Installer:**
   185→   - Double-click the downloaded `android-studio-<version>-windows.exe` file
   186→   - If prompted by User Account Control (UAC), click "Yes" to allow installation
   187→
   188→2. **Follow the Setup Wizard:**
   189→   - Click "Next" on the welcome screen
   190→   - **Choose Components:**
   191→     - ✅ Android Studio
   192→     - ✅ Android Virtual Device (recommended)
   193→   - **Choose Install Location:**
   194→     - Default: `C:\Program Files\Android\Android Studio`
   195→     - Ensure at least 3 GB of free space
   196→   - **Choose Start Menu Folder:** Accept default or customize
   197→   - Click "Install" and wait for completion
   198→   - Click "Finish" and launch Android Studio
   199→
   200→3. **Complete First Launch Setup:**
   201→   - Select "Do not import settings" (for first-time installation)
   202→   - Click "OK"
   203→   - **Android Studio Setup Wizard** will start:
   204→     - Click "Next" on the welcome screen
   205→     - **Install Type:** Select "Standard" (recommended)
   206→       - Standard installs Android SDK, Android SDK Platform, and Android Virtual Device
   207→     - **Select UI Theme:** Choose "Light" or "Darcula" (your preference)
   208→     - **Verify Settings:** Review the components to be downloaded
   209→       - Android SDK Location: `C:\Users\<YourUsername>\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk`
   210→       - SDK Components: ~5 GB download
   211→     - Click "Finish" to begin downloading
   212→     - Wait for SDK components to download and install (10-30 minutes depending on connection)
   213→   - Click "Finish" when complete
   214→
   215→##### macOS Installation
   216→
   217→1. **Mount the DMG Image:**
   218→   - Double-click the downloaded `android-studio-<version>-mac.dmg` or `android-studio-<version>-mac_arm.dmg` file
   219→   - A window will open showing the Android Studio icon
   220→
   221→2. **Install Android Studio:**
   222→   - Drag and drop the **Android Studio** icon into the **Applications** folder
   223→   - Wait for the copy operation to complete
   224→   - Eject the DMG image
   225→
   226→3. **Launch Android Studio:**
   227→   - Open the **Applications** folder
   228→   - Double-click **Android Studio**

1→# Create a development environment setup guide
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→There are no instructions for setting up a development environment. The project has no package.json, build.gradle, or any dependency manifest file, and no documentation explaining what tools, SDKs, or runtimes are needed. A new developer cloning this repo would find an empty project with no guidance on how to bootstrap development — what IDE to use, what SDK versions are required, or how to create the initial project scaffold.
     6→
     7→## Rationale
     8→
     9→Developer onboarding friction is the biggest early-project risk. Without setup documentation, each new contributor wastes time figuring out prerequisites independently. For an Android/APK project, setup is particularly important due to Android SDK versioning, emulator configuration, and build tool requirements. This documentation should be created alongside or immediately after tech stack decisions.

  2721→   - Select branch to push (e.g., `main`)
  2722→   - Click **Push**

  2724→**Via Command Line:**

  2726→```bash

  2729→
  2730→# Verify remote

  2746→### Troubleshooting Project Initialization
  2747→
  2748→#### Issue: Gradle Sync Failed
  2749→
  2750→**Symptoms:**
  2751→- "Gradle sync failed" error in Android Studio
  2752→- Build errors in "Build" tool window
  2753→- Red underlines in build.gradle.kts files
  2754→
  2755→**Common Causes and Solutions:**
  2756→
  2757→**1. Network Issues (Cannot Download Dependencies)**
  2758→
  2759→**Solution:**
  2760→- Check internet connection
  2761→- If behind proxy, configure proxy in Android Studio:
  2762→  - **File** → **Settings** → **Appearance & Behavior** → **System Settings** → **HTTP Proxy**
  2763→  - Configure proxy settings
  2764→  - Click **Check connection** to test
  2765→  - Click **OK** and sync again
  2766→
  2767→**2. Incompatible Gradle/Plugin Versions**
  2768→
  2769→**Solution:**
  2770→- Verify compatible versions:
  2771→  - Gradle 9.4.1 requires Android Gradle Plugin (AGP) 9.0+
  2772→  - Check `gradle/libs.versions.toml` for AGP version
  2773→- Update if needed:
  2774→  ```toml
  2775→  [versions]
  2776→  agp = "9.1.0"  # Update to latest stable version
  2777→  ```
  2778→- Sync again
  2779→
  2780→**3. Corrupted Gradle Cache**
  2781→
  2782→**Solution:**
  2783→```bash
  2784→# Delete Gradle cache
  2785→# Windows
  2786→rd /s /q %USERPROFILE%\.gradle\caches
  2787→
  2788→# macOS/Linux
  2789→rm -rf ~/.gradle/caches
  2790→
  2791→# Rebuild project
  2792→./gradlew clean build --refresh-dependencies
  2793→```
  2794→
  2795→**4. JDK Version Mismatch**
  2796→
  2797→**Solution:**
  2798→- Ensure JDK 17 or higher is configured
  2799→- In Android Studio: **File** → **Project Structure** → **SDK Location**
  2800→- Set **Gradle JDK** to **Embedded JDK (17)** or higher
  2801→- Click **OK** and sync again
  2802→
  2803→#### Issue: "SDK location not found"
  2804→
  2805→**Symptom:**
  2806→- Error: "SDK location not found. Define location with sdk.dir in the local.properties file..."
  2807→
  2808→**Solution:**
  2809→1. **Create/Update `local.properties`:**
  2810→   ```properties
  2811→   # Windows
  2812→   sdk.dir=C\:\\Users\\<YourUsername>\\AppData\\Local\\Android\\Sdk
  2813→
  2814→   # macOS
  2815→   sdk.dir=/Users/<YourUsername>/Library/Android/sdk
  2816→
  2817→   # Linux
  2818→   sdk.dir=/home/<YourUsername>/Android/Sdk
  2819→   ```
  2820→
  2821→2. **Or set via Android Studio:**
  2822→   - **File** → **Project Structure** → **SDK Location**
  2823→   - Browse to SDK location
  2824→   - Click **OK**
  2825→
  2826→#### Issue: "This project requires a newer version of the Android Gradle plugin"
  2827→
  2828→**Symptom:**
  2829→- Error message recommending AGP upgrade
  2830→
  2831→**Solution:**
  2832→1. **Update AGP version in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`:**
  2833→   ```toml
  2834→   [versions]
  2835→   agp = "9.1.0"  # Update to recommended version
  2836→   ```
  2837→
  2838→2. **Sync Gradle files**
  2839→
  2840→3. **Update Gradle wrapper if needed:**

  2842→   ./gradlew wrapper --gradle-version=9.4.1
  2843→   ```

  3036→#### Run Application on Emulator
  3037→
  3038→**Method 1: Via Android Studio (Quick Launch)**
  3039→
  3040→1. **Ensure Project is Built:**
  3041→   - Click **Make Project** icon or press `Ctrl + F9` / `Cmd + F9`
  3042→
  3043→2. **Select Target Device:**
  3044→   - Click device dropdown in toolbar (next to Run button)
  3045→   - Select emulator from list (e.g., **Pixel_8_API_35**)
  3046→
  3047→3. **Run Application:**
  3048→   - Click **Run** button (green play icon) or press `Shift + F10` / `Ctrl + R`
  3049→   - Or navigate to **Run** → **Run 'app'**
  3050→
  3051→**Expected Behavior:**
  3052→1. Emulator launches (if not already running)
  3053→2. Android OS boots up (may take 30-60 seconds on first launch)
  3054→3. Application installs automatically
  3055→4. Application launches on emulator
  3056→
  3057→**Method 2: Via Command Line with Gradle**
  3058→
  3059→1. **Start Emulator:**
  3060→   ```bash
  3061→   # List available AVDs
  3062→   emulator -list-avds
  3063→
  3064→   # Start specific emulator

  3066→
  3067→   # Start in background (no GUI window, headless)

  3131→
  3132→### Running on Physical Device
  3133→
  3134→Running on a physical device provides real-world performance testing and access to actual hardware features.
  3135→
  3136→#### Prerequisites
  3137→
  3138→- Android device running Android 7.0 (API 24) or higher
  3139→- USB cable (USB-C or Micro-USB depending on device)
  3140→- USB debugging enabled on device
  3141→
  3142→#### Step 1: Enable Developer Options on Device
  3143→
  3144→Developer Options are hidden by default on Android devices.
  3145→
  3146→**Enable Developer Options:**
  3147→
  3148→1. Open **Settings** on your Android device
  3149→2. Navigate to **About Phone** (may be under **System** → **About Phone**)
  3150→3. Find **Build Number** (may be under **Software Information**)
  3151→4. Tap **Build Number** 7 times rapidly
  3152→5. You'll see a message: "You are now a developer!"
  3153→
  3154→**Expected Result:**
  3155→- **Developer Options** menu appears in Settings
  3156→
  3157→#### Step 2: Enable USB Debugging
  3158→
  3159→**Enable USB Debugging:**
  3160→
  3161→1. Open **Settings** on device
  3162→2. Navigate to **System** → **Developer Options**
  3163→   - On some devices: **Settings** → **Developer Options**
  3164→3. Enable **Developer Options** toggle (if present)
  3165→4. Scroll down and enable **USB Debugging**
  3166→5. Confirm prompt: "Allow USB debugging?"
  3167→
  3168→**Optional but Recommended Settings:**
  3169→
  3170→While in Developer Options, also enable:
  3171→- **Stay Awake** - Screen stays on while charging (useful during development)
  3172→- **Install via USB** - Allow app installs via USB (Android 11+)
  3173→
  3174→#### Step 3: Connect Device to Computer
  3175→
  3176→1. **Connect Device:**
  3177→   - Connect device to computer using USB cable
  3178→   - Unlock device screen
  3179→
  3180→2. **Select USB Mode:**
  3181→   - When device is connected, you'll see USB notification
  3182→   - Tap notification and select:
  3183→     - **File Transfer (MTP)** or
  3184→     - **PTP** or
  3185→     - **USB Tethering**
  3186→   - (Default "Charging only" mode won't work for development)
  3187→
  3188→3. **Authorize Computer:**
  3189→   - Device will show prompt: "Allow USB debugging from this computer?"
  3190→   - Check "Always allow from this computer"
  3191→   - Tap **Allow** or **OK**
  3192→
  3193→**Expected Result:**
  3194→- Device is recognized by computer
  3195→- No longer shows "unauthorized" in ADB
  3196→
  3197→#### Step 4: Verify Device Connection
  3198→
  3199→**Via Android Studio:**
  3200→1. Open Device Manager: **Tools** → **Device Manager**
  3201→2. Device should appear under **Physical** tab
  3202→3. Shows device model and Android version
  3203→
  3204→**Via Command Line:**
  3205→```bash
  3206→adb devices
  3207→```
  3208→
  3209→**Expected Output:**

  3211→List of devices attached
  3212→ABC123DEF456    device
  3213→```
  3214→
  3215→**If device shows as "unauthorized":**
  3216→- Unlock device screen
  3217→- Check for authorization prompt on device
  3218→- Re-enable USB debugging if needed
  3219→
  3220→**If device is not detected:**
  3221→- Try different USB cable (some cables are charge-only)
  3222→- Try different USB port on computer
  3223→- Install device-specific USB drivers (Windows) - see [Troubleshooting: Device Not Detected](#issue-physical-device-not-detected)
  3224→
  3225→#### Step 5: Run Application on Physical Device
  3226→
  3227→**Method 1: Via Android Studio**
  3228→
  3229→1. **Select Physical Device:**
  3230→   - Click device dropdown in toolbar
  3231→   - Select your physical device (e.g., "Samsung Galaxy S23")
  3232→
  3233→2. **Run Application:**
  3234→   - Click **Run** button or press `Shift + F10` / `Ctrl + R`
  3235→   - Or navigate to **Run** → **Run 'app'**
  3236→
  3237→**Expected Behavior:**
  3238→1. Application builds
  3239→2. APK installs on device
  3240→3. Application launches on device
  3241→4. Device screen shows app running
  3242→
  3243→**Method 2: Via Command Line**
  3244→
  3245→```bash

  3247→./gradlew installDebug
  3248→

  3250→adb shell am start -n com.yourcompany.inventory/.MainActivity
  3251→```
  3252→
  3253→**Method 3: Install Pre-built APK**
  3254→
  3255→```bash
  3256→# Install APK on connected device
  3257→adb install app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk
  3258→
  3259→# If already installed, reinstall
  3260→adb install -r app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk
  3261→```
  3262→
  3263→#### Managing Multiple Devices
  3264→
  3265→If multiple devices/emulators are connected:
  3266→
  3267→**List Devices:**
  3268→```bash

  3270→```
  3271→
  3272→**Output Example:**

  3274→List of devices attached

1→# Create a development environment setup guide
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→There are no instructions for setting up a development environment. The project has no package.json, build.gradle, or any dependency manifest file, and no documentation explaining what tools, SDKs, or runtimes are needed. A new developer cloning this repo would find an empty project with no guidance on how to bootstrap development — what IDE to use, what SDK versions are required, or how to create the initial project scaffold.
     6→
     7→## Rationale
     8→
     9→Developer onboarding friction is the biggest early-project risk. Without setup documentation, each new contributor wastes time figuring out prerequisites independently. For an Android/APK project, setup is particularly important due to Android SDK versioning, emulator configuration, and build tool requirements. This documentation should be created alongside or immediately after tech stack decisions.
    10→
    11→---
    12→*This spec was created from ideation and is pending detailed specification.*
    13→

# Android Development Environment Setup Guide

Complete guide for setting up your Android development environment for this project.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
   - [Operating System Requirements](#operating-system-requirements)
   - [Hardware Requirements](#hardware-requirements)
   - [Software Requirements](#software-requirements)
2. [Android SDK Installation](#android-sdk-installation)
3. [Android Studio Setup](#android-studio-setup)
4. [Project Initialization](#project-initialization)
5. [Building and Running](#building-and-running)
6. [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting)
7. [Additional Resources](#additional-resources)

---

## Prerequisites

Before you begin setting up your Android development environment, ensure your system meets the following requirements.

### Operating System Requirements

This project supports development on the following operating systems:

#### Windows
- **Version:** Windows 10 (64-bit) or Windows 11 (64-bit)
- **Notes:**
  - 32-bit Windows is not supported
  - Windows 7 and 8 are not supported

#### macOS
- **Version:** macOS 11.0 (Big Sur) or higher
- **Supported Architectures:**
  - Intel-based Macs
  - Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) Macs
- **Notes:** Hardware acceleration is built-in via macOS Hypervisor Framework

#### Linux
- **Distributions:**
  - Ubuntu 20.04 LTS or higher (recommended)
  - Other distributions with GNOME or KDE desktop
- **Architecture:** 64-bit distribution capable of running 32-bit applications
- **Notes:**
  - Debian-based distributions are best supported
  - Requires additional 32-bit libraries for Android SDK compatibility
